summary: Feedback on the modern theme

description.text: ### Tested versions 4.6 dev3 ### System information Godot v4.5.1.stable - TUXEDO OS 24.04.3 LTS on Wayland - X11 display driver, Multi-window, 2 monitors - OpenGL 3 (Compatibility) - Mesa Intel(R) Iris(R) Xe Graphics (RPL-P) - 13th Gen Intel(R) Core(TM) i7-13700H (20 threads) - 62.52 GiB memory ### Issue description Though I do think the new theme looks nice, there are a couple of readability issues that I noticed after using it for a few days. Some of those might be deliberate choices, in which case they can be disregarded. I'm one of those who do struggle with colors, especially in low contrasts, so these points might not look the same for everybody else as they do for me or other colorblind people. I'm aware that the classic look is still available, and switching to it does fix those issue for me. But the idea here, is not to argue if one is better than the other, or how easy it is to switch, but rather to give feedback on the actual modern theme, so it keeps the editor appealing to new comers, but also to make it more readable for everyone who wishes to keep using it, especially those with bad eye sight. Here are the points, in no particular order : 1 - The guidelines in the Scene and Filesystem tabs are inconsistent. Sometimes they're there, some times they are not, depending on what's selected, even if you moved away from the tab an now working on another area of the editor. In the screenshot, there are no guidelines in the Scene tree, but there is one the Filesystem tab, even if I'm not actively working on any of them. On the classic theme, the guidelines are always there, but the line is highlighted for the selected line. This makes more sense to me, as the guidelines do not pop in and out of existence. I actually though it was buggy when I was playing around with the editor. Suggestion here to pick one and roll with it, either show the guidelines, or not. 2 - The padding on the menus is way too bulky. I think a bunch of pixels could be trimmed here. 3 - There is no distinction between the menu, and the text editor here. 4 - Text and icons are way to washed off compared to their background, this is by for the most troublesome to me, given my color problem, and also the most present through out the editor. Suggestion here is to slightly lighten the text and icons towards white. I do thing there is quite some margin before it pops off too much and starts to look bad 5 - It's hard to say where the editor ends and where the animation panel begins here, though I think there is this proposal to address that : https://github.com/godotengine/godot-proposals/issues/13581 6 - Either the text in the tabs is to small, or the tabs themselves are too big. Suggestion here it to either make the text a bit bigger, bolder (better readability), or at least reduce the size of tabs a bit, which won't improve the readability, but should (subjectively) look better. Check the Blender tabs (Layout, modelling, sculpting) ... ### Steps to reproduce N/A ### Minimal reproduction project (MRP) N/A

summary: QoL improvements for Resource Duplication in the Inspector

description.text: followup to https://github.com/godotengine/godot/pull/109458. Closes https://github.com/godotengine/godot-proposals/issues/13163 Some quirks from the PR were pushed to be addressed at a later date. For instance, making a Resource from multiple Nodes unique, or paths showing only the Resource UID being targeted as an external Resource. These don't _break_ the usability of #109458 (the latter, for example, is fixed as soon as the Resource is saved), but are some UX issues that even get me at times. So, in order to deal with this, here's the changes: ## Multi-Node Fixes ### Before ![MultiBefore](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/a3a8f21d-f81f-47c5-bd1d-b97709e1aec8) ### After ![MultiAfter](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/ae7c2ad0-1e43-48be-b3a4-ebef501a95ab) - Every Resource from a selection will be duplicated and assigned to its respective node. This ensures the same behavior as selecting each Node one by one and making its Resource unique. - For `Make_Unique (Recursive)`, it triggers an Error message if you uncheck a Parent Resource but tries to duplicate its Subresources. This is because duplication has no effect in these cases (i.e, SubResources are still linked). This is fine when only one Node is selected, but it crashes for Dictionaries, so that's why it must be addressed in this PR. ## Subresource paths - Subresources whose path only consists of UIDs are correctly categorized as internal to a scene, therefore using the appropriate description for the tooltip, ensuring clarity in the process.

summary: Fix control set_position modifying anchor rect size as a side effect

description.text:
Time for the 3rd attempt at this with great concern of another regression.

- Fixes #100536 
- Fixes #100626

Does not recreate:
- #105771
- #106477

Different approach from:
- #112525

Just for clarity: I'm gonna call the rect formed by anchor points + anchor offsets as the "anchor rect".

These changes continue the work from #104357 and #106141 with the answer that if the cache isn't reliable, then just compute it. The only time the anchor rect's top left differs from the control position is if the control's min size is larger than the size of the anchor rect and grow directions aren't set to end, so just check for that the same way it's check in `_size_changed()`.

This solution just makes the workflow of setting up the anchor rect as a size constraint easier since something that sounds like it shouldn't have an effect on size, `set_position()`, will not impact size changes. It also makes moving a control after meticulously setting up offsets in custom anchor preset less annoying since a user no longer has to readjust offsets after moving. Ultimately, this helps min size, grow direction, and anchor rect be more independent settings for how a control is positioned and sized.

This doesn't provide a universal way of always keeping a control at its min size, which is more of the intent of the original bug reports, but does make it easier. A user would still need to have the anchor points and anchor offsets set so that the anchor rect is size 0,0 to always stay at min size. The main caveat to this is that using anchor presets via inspector sets different offsets based on the current min size. This means switching between the presets in inspector can change sizing behavior, but whether that's desirable whole different discussion and the user can just switch to the custom preset. 

Despite the indirect solution, the changes end up fixing the bugs because the containers in question have a 0,0 min size without children. It'd still be possible for containers to not always be their min size if it had children and then had it's presets changed as described above.

## Tests a plenty
Because of the history of regressions, I made a bunch of tests around positioning controls. I don't quite understand how right-to-left works, so I wouldn't be surprised if the rtl test case doesn't make sense. 

- `[SceneTree][Control] Set position with grow direction` tests cover #105771
- `[SceneTree][Control] Set position with grow direction in rtl` tries to cover #106477

summary: Change `TileSetScenesCollectionSource` raw pointers in the TileSet editor to ref

description.text:
…tor to Ref<TileSetScenesCollectionSource>

### Problem:
* Addresses Fixes #112643
* The Editor crashes due to a attempting to access a dangling pointer to a released TileSetScenesCollectionSource.
* The resource is released by the TileSetEditor either through Undo or by deleting it using the Editor UI.
* The pointers in tile_set_scenes_collection_source_editor are now dangling since they are not updated.
* Note - The pointers are only invalid after the redo stack is cleared and there aren't any other actions holding on to a reference of the source collection.

### Solution:
* Suggestion of @kleonc and @groud.
* Made tile_set_scenes_collection_source a Ref instead of a raw point, protecting against dangling pointers when the source is released.
   * Updated the relevant code to support usage of a Ref instead of a raw pointer.

### Testing:
* I verified that the repro steps provided in the issue no longer cause a crash.
* I verified that using the delete button in the UI still successfulyl deletes the source collection.
* I stress tested the undo redo stack by creating multiple sources and spamming undo\redo

summary: When Base Spacing < 3, screen moves a few pixels every time a Control is selected or deselected

description.text: ### Tested versions - Reproducible in v4.5.1.stable.flathub [f62fdbde1] ### System information Godot v4.5.1.stable (f62fdbde1) - Freedesktop SDK 25.08 (Flatpak runtime) on Wayland - X11 display driver, Multi-window, 2 monitors - OpenGL 3 (Compatibility) - Mesa Intel(R) HD Graphics 5500 (BDW GT2) - Intel(R) Core(TM) i5-5300U CPU @ 2.30GHz (4 threads) - 10.59 GiB memory ### Issue description In Editor settings, when `interface/theme/base_spacing` is set to 2 or less, there's a GUI issue where the screen e.g. in 2D mode keeps shifting 1 or a few pixels every time a node like a control is selected or deselected. It took me a while to figure out this setting is the cause - it seems to be caused by the additional buttons that appear in the toolbar when certain node types are selected. I set it to 0 here to make the issue more visible: Again, the issue is not really the toolbar being resized, but how it causes the screen below to be resized and shift a pixel down every selection and up again every deselection. This makes the setting unusable, which is very disappointing, as I want to set it to 2 because I really dislike extra unnecessary space being wasted in GUI. I want small buttons to maximize my useful work area. To solve this, you can either: - (preferably) fix these additional buttons so they respect the setting and not change the spacing, or - fix the logic so toolbar resizes don't make the screen below it shift (e.g. by compensating the shift somehow), or - if not possible, make the toolbar a fixed size by the max height, i.e. it always has space for the selection-specific buttons even when they're not there, so it doesn't resize based on what is or isn't there ### Steps to reproduce 1. In Editor settings, set `interface/theme/base_spacing` to 2 or less (set to 0 to make the issue more visible). 2. Open a scene with control nodes in 2D view mode. 3. You may want to have a background image to see the issue better. Press Shift+F to frame-resize to it. 4. Select a control node and deselect it to see the screen editing pan/position shift by 1 pixel (if set to 2) or more (if set to 0). Note how this is caused by the toolbar being resized. If set to 3 or more, the issue doesn't seem to happen. ### Minimal reproduction project (MRP) N/A

summary: Optimize the bresenham algorithm to avoid an unneeded vector allocation

description.text:
When I implemented the bresenham algorithm 4 year ago, I didn't really put a lot of effort into optimizing it. This is now fixed for internal use, as this new implementation does not allocate a `Vector<Point2i>` anymore. I assume this should improve performance a bit in the TileMap editor when drawing lines.

Instead, this PR implements a C++ iterable that can be used that way:
```
for (Vector2i point : Geometry2D::Bresenham(from, to)) {
    ...
}
```

Note that we have tests for the algorithm that all passed correctly. So I think we can safely assume this new implementation is working fine.

summary: Fix crash after clearing `SpriteFrames`

description.text:
- *Fixes: https://github.com/godotengine/godot/issues/112576*

## Issue Description

When clearing `SpriteFrames` for `AnimatedSprite2D`, the relevant `EditorPlugin` did not hide immediately. When clicking anywhere of `EditorPlugin`, the editor will crash.

## Explaination

The `SpriteFramesEditor` observes the `sprite_frames_changed` signal of `AnimatedSprite2D`. But it didn't hide `EditorPlugin` in `_edit()` when clearing.

https://github.com/godotengine/godot/blob/e6aa06d3de372513bedb036d2adb1052a9b4b87f/editor/scene/sprite_frames_editor_plugin.cpp#L1962

 As described in https://github.com/godotengine/godot/pull/110280 , the bottom panel first `edit()`, then `make_visible(false)`, to hide the bottom panel plugin.

But in this scenario, `EditorInspector` called `SpriteFramesEditor::_edit()` directly, it didnot call `make_visible(false)`, so `EditorPlugin` is still there. But relevant `SpriteFrames` had been freed, so redraw caused crash.

## Solution

We should hide `EditorPlugin` when clearing `SpriteFrames` of `AnimatedSprite2D`, then editor won't crash.

[spriteframes.webm](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/6a72aaac-0a3a-45e7-bcdc-a8c718fd57b8)

## MRP

[test-112576.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/23523160/test-112576.zip)

summary: Add hints for `Translation`

description.text:
This PR is separated into 3 commits for easier review.

## 1. Add hints for `Translation`

There are two problems.

First, whether a message in `Translation` should be translated using `get_message()` or `get_plural_message()` depends on whether it was added using `add_message()` or `add_plural_message()`. But there is currently no way to know that information in code.

Second, even if we record the source of the message when adding it, we still cannot properly call `get_plural_message()`: The plural form of the source text remains unknown. This also means that we can't reliably restore a `Translation` loaded from PO files back to a PO file. For example, #106573 attempts to convert PO files to MO files on export, but it has to write `?` for the `msgid_plural` field, which is technically wrong.

Considering https://github.com/godotengine/godot-proposals/issues/7334, it'll also be better if metadata like comments and locations can be stored along with the message. The long-term goal is to make PO, CSV, and `Translation` interchangeable.

This PR added `{get,set}_hint()` for storing hints like untranslated plural, comments, and locations (references to the program's source code).

How a `Translation` is serialized to a dictionary (`_{get,set}_messages()`) is also updated:

For messages, keys can be either:

- `&"msgid"`
- `[&"&msgctxt", &"msgid"]`

Their values can be either:

- `&"msgstr[0]"`
- `[&"msgstr[0], &"msgstr[1]", ...]`

The optional hints are stored using an integer key. The value is another dictionary, mapping message keys to a hints dictionary which maps `HINT_*` to the hint value. Empty values are not stored for hints.

This makes the format compact. And it's still compatible with older versions as long as new features (context, plural forms, and hints) are not used.

## 2. Use plural hint when importing/exporting `Translation`s

When importing CSV files and loading PO files, `HINT_PLURAL` is now set. For exisitng projects, `.csv` files have to be reimported to get this hint.

When generating the translation template, the parse result is now a `Translation` instead of a vector of custom structs. This lays the foundation to adding template generation related APIs in the future (for problems like #111074).

When implementing, I also fixed some problems about the `Translation` APIs:

- Added missing `has_message()`
- Changed internal version of `get_message_list()` to yield `MessageKey`s instead of `StringName`s. The scripting API version concats `msgid` and `msgctxt` for compatibility.

Usages of other hints (comments and locations) are not added in this PR. I think that should be done in separate ones.

- They are information for the translators, so not as important as the plural hint.
- The PO parsing code is a complex state machine, hard to change.
- The CSV importer does not have the concept of comments and locations yet. CSV template generation produces these two metadata as ignored columns.

## 3. Add translation viewer

Currently, the inspector only shows `Locale` and `Plural Rules Override` for `Translation`s. This commit adds a View Messages button for inspecting contents in the resource.

This makes it easier to debug translation related problems and dogfoods the new API.

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/a4e1d4c2-9c3d-4a38-81c2-019e9a780b0e

*The screencast is using the Classic style theme. `Tree`s don't have frames when using the modern style theme, which makes many editor UI look broken :(

When implementing, I also added `get_plural_forms()` to `Translation` for getting all possible plural forms for a given message.

Test project: [translation-hints.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/23522279/translation-hints.zip)

summary: Small Improvements to the `Make Unique (Recursive)` Popup

description.text: Since #109458 has been merged, I took the time to make some adjustments in the `Make Unique (Recursive)` popup. One leftover from the same PR was how unique Resources could still be duplicated when opening the popup. After Adding that, I realized some improvements on readability could be made, such as adding column titles, and tooltips to explain why a Resource couldn't be duplicated. Also, for custom resouces, Godot Scripts were cluttering the UI, since they by default cannot be made unique in the popup. With that in mind, I'd like to show how it currently looks: | Before | After | | ------------- | ------------- | | | ![After](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/2af91a02-c40c-43ca-a467-bf9d1c4891bb) | - Root's property name now shows up in the popup - Scripts now removed, showing Resources that matters to the user (i.e, which ones they would actually duplicate). Do note that Shaders still show up, like previously. - Disabling the checkbox if Resource is either unique or has `PROPERTY_USAGE_NEVER_DUPLICATE` - Tooltip descriptions as to why they couldn't be made unique. - Column titles to make it prettier Finally, this further implements https://github.com/godotengine/godot-proposals/issues/10319, since there's now an indicator when using both the inspector and the popup that a Resource cannot be duplicated. Thanks!

Search results can keep original information if contents have been modified such as line number, matches count. 
Search results might also contain users' operation traces such as vertical scroll position, highlighted item.
If rebuilding the search tree, these old information will be lost.

On the other hand, there may be many search results (introduced by #104676 ), when the user changes the theme, each of which will rebuild the search tree causing the engine to stutter, maybe.

summary: Button icons in inspector are either crisp or blurry depending on nesting level using modern theme

description.text: ### Tested versions - Reproducible in 4.6-dev ### System information Ubuntu 24.04.2 LTS ### Issue description When using the modern theme, with the compact setting, on a 1080p display, the icons that are present on EditorProperty entries can be either blurry or crisp depending on the nesting level. The icons in green are shown correctly (nesting level X) The icons in orange are shown blurry (nesting level X + 1, with a section in-between). Because of this variable result, this may not be fixed on the control itself. I think there is an issue with some of the margins or paddings that can be uneven between the nesting levels. This does not happen on the classic theme. In the `theme_modern.cpp` file, I noticed that non-integer multiplications of the scale factor are used in many places (example: `1.5 * EDSCALE`), whereas the classic theme only uses integers. A rounding of the resulting value using `Math::floor`, `Math::round`, or `Math::ceil` is rarely done. With the trend of having more high-DPI screens on laptops, I can imagine that testing on standard 1080p resolution can be unpractical. But this still should be cared about in some way, as not all machines comfortably handle external monitors with high resolutions, especially modest ones. ### Steps to reproduce Use a 1920x1080 monitor or any similar where the OS scaling is 100%. No Hidpi or Retina. Create a new project. Create a 2D scene. Add a Sprite2D and an AnimationPlayer (so that the key buttons appear). Set the editor theme to modern, with the compact preset. Inspect the Sprite2D. You can observe the result like the screenshot above. ### Minimal reproduction project (MRP) N/A

summary: Make inspector property buttons (keyframe, revert...) trigger on mouse release, use theme for icon colors

description.text:
Fixes #112561

This change fixes the contextual buttons behavior on parts of the inspector and project/editor settings:
- the keyframe, revert, delete buttons and checkbox on `EditorProperty` (checkbox visible on theme overrides, delete visible on custom metadata)
- the keyframe button and Enable checkbox on `EditorInspectorSection`

List of corrections done in this commit:
- Buttons and checkboxes now wait for a mouse button up event to trigger, instead of triggering on the mouse button down alone. This is standard GUI behavior on discrete action buttons, and gives one last chance to "escape" before triggering the action by dragging away before releasing the mouse button.
- Button icons use the actual editor theme colors, instead of hardcoded values.
- The pressed state while the mouse button is held down is added, according to the editor theme.
- On EditorProperty, the button area is made non-draggable.

summary: Add a project setting to break on any error or warning printed

description.text: - Alternative implementation of https://github.com/godotengine/godot/pull/50955 that is more general-purpose. This can be used to find and resolve errors/warnings as they occur during development, which makes them less likely to pop up at the last minute. This affects any error/warning message regardless of its source (engine, `push_error()`/`push_warning()` in scripts, ...). This is **disabled by default**, and must be enabled in the Project Settings to work: - This closes https://github.com/godotengine/godot-proposals/issues/1729. **Testing project:** [test_error_break.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/23506416/test_error_break.zip) ## Preview https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/57102847-4163-4353-82ef-45431d4c64f9 ## TODO - [ ] Do not break for errors that already break without the setting, such as the method call on null instance error seen in the above video. (This is why it's breaking twice on that message.) - [ ] Implement in LocalDebugger for command-line debugging. I've attempted this in the past, but it seemed to not work reliably (it would either not break or break twice in a row). - [ ] Use an approach that's functional in C#/GDExtension as well, and works when the GDScript module is disabled at compile-time. - I could call `EngineDebugger::get_script_debugger()->debug(nullptr, true, false);`, but it doesn't seem to do anything in GDScript. - [ ] Add a way to exclude certain error/warning messages, e.g. based on a regex. During engine development on `master`, you may run into unavoidable errors you don't want to silence using `Engine.print_error_enabled = false` as it's too general.

summary: Shader instance uniform colors black on web export but not otherwise using compatibility rederer

description.text:
### Tested versions

4.5.1

### System information

Godot v4.5.1.stable - macOS Sequoia (15.6.1) - Multi-window, 2 monitors - OpenGL 3 (Compatibility) - Apple M2 Pro - Apple M2 Pro (10 threads) - 16.00 GiB memory

### Issue description

With instance uniforms now supported in Compatibility mode (https://github.com/godotengine/godot/pull/96819) I decided to try this in a prototype I'm working on. It worked as expected while working in the editor and running on the computer. However, when I tested making a web export to share a majority of the instance uniform materials rendered as if the instance color uniform was set to black when viewed on the web. 

It appears the issue occurs if you add a sufficient number of geometry instances (>20-ish?) using instance uniforms. At this point the last X instances will be the only ones rendering correctly, while all previous ones added will render as if the uniform color was black. Maybe some (quite low?) limit in how many instance uniforms can be set to the buffer that the tests in the original PR didn't encounter as they only used a couple of geometry instances?

Test scene on compatibility running from editor on macOS:
![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/8143b394-c7d5-427a-b142-0488f5621bfd)

Same scene exported running on web:
![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/b588dbc5-3012-4933-9a5a-b267854c38b8)



### Steps to reproduce

So, I put together a simple test project to reproduce this:

- Make a 3d scene and add a mesh instance capsule, a camera to view it and a light to light it.
- Add a new shader material to the mesh instance and add a new visual shader to this material
- In the visual shader, add a instance uniform for the color. I also added a little shininess to see the shader material was actually being used even when the instance uniform color was not.

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/9ef4568a-78e6-465b-8373-eeebef3cd8e5)

- Duplicate the capsule mesh a number of times and set different colors on the mesh instances to see it's working as expected.

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/99a83ceb-87dc-4983-8d26-c3799bbe2d69)

- Run and see that it also displays as expected when run. 

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/2608e211-3d36-4e5d-b9ff-ed8e9cff494d)

- Export for web and run and see that a lot of the capsules are now black.

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/cbead1f0-c877-4996-86bb-e854f7128eec)

- Reduce the number of capsules in the scene and export again, run on the web and see that the problem has gone away.

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/24602f43-c3e5-45b6-bf00-2b742966a39c)

[web-instance-uniforms.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/23503327/web-instance-uniforms.zip)

summary: Screen readers fail to recognize UI elements when I build the engine myself

description.text: ### Tested versions v4.6.dev.custom_build [e47fb8b89] (the test build I cobbled together for this report) v4.5.1.stable.mono.conservatory_edition [6d6165f55] (my own fork, see issue description) ### System information Godot v4.6.dev (e47fb8b89) - Windows 10 (build 19045) - Multi-window, 3 monitors - Vulkan (Forward+) - dedicated NVIDIA GeForce RTX 3070 Ti (NVIDIA; 32.0.15.8108) - Intel(R) Core(TM) i9-10900K CPU @ 3.70GHz (20 threads) - 31.85 GiB memory ### Issue description **For some vital context ahead of time:** * I first noted this issue in my own build of the engine, **which is running on 4.5.1-stable. The issue was first noted when my fork was based on 4.5, however.** * At that time, I chose to not report the issue, because I only noticed it in my fork. Accessibility tools functioned as intended when I used versions of the engine I downloaded from the website. * **Vitally,** I did not ever touch accessibility code at all in my fork, so this bug was a bit strange to me! Zoom forward to today, where I need to focus heavily on accessibility as part of my UI cleanup process. Just kind of by chance, I got a bit of a hunch: What would happen if I built the engine plain and stock, right here, right now? Lo and behold, after building the repo (as of [e47fb8b89]), narrators and other screen readers have no context into what's going on inside of the game window, the same exact issue I noticed in my own fork. To complete the test, I downloaded 4.6-dev3 from the website, and just like last time, it *does* function in the officially provided build. To really triple check, I had a look with Accessibility Insights for Windows which can debug accessibility nodes in any application. **`v4.6.dev.custom_build` and `v4.5.1.stable.mono.conservatory_edition`:** Erroneous; Nothing shows up and the game is just considered to be a pane. *** **`v4.6-dev3` (from website under experimental builds):** Correct; Buttons in the game window are registered as accessibility objects. *** I know this issue isn't exactly the most useful as it's basically a reverse *"It works on my machine"*, but I figured it was reproducible enough to the point where I ought to just try to report it anyway. ### Steps to reproduce The steps I did are as follows: 1. Open Visual Studio 2022 2. Click the "Clone a Repository..." button on the right side of the landing pad. 3. Enter this repository's git URL `https://github.com/godotengine/godot.git` 4. Clone the repo, let it finish downloading and such 5. Exit Visual Studio 2022 6. Open Powershell or Command Prompt in the repo directory 7. Execute `scons platform=windows` and wait for completion 8. Open `bin` directory and run the engine 9. Create a blank project, and a new scene for UI. 10. Add a button to the blank UI, give it some text. 11. Open the Windows Narrator or other accessibility application of your choosing. 12. Save the UI scene and set it as the startup scene. Run the project. 13. The narrator and other apps will be unable to recognize elements within the game window as accessibility objects, and won't read them aloud. ### Minimal reproduction project (MRP) [accessibility-bug-report.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/23499739/accessibility-bug-report.zip) \* This contains the scene outlined in the reproduction, just with two buttons so that I could focus them with tab and the arrow keys.

summary: `CharacterBody*D.get_gravity()` only considers changes to area overrides when moving

description.text:
### Tested versions

Godot 4.5 stable

### System information

### Issue description

CharacterBody3D.get_gravity() does NOT take account of the gravity settings in overlapping Area3D.

1. Just create a scene and put a CharacterBody3d with a CollisionShape into it. Without anything else, its get_gravity() returns (0, -9.8, 0) which is the default gravity setting in the project;
2. Now add an Area3D with a CollisionShape which overlaps with the character. Set Space Override in Graivty for the Area3D with another value;
3. You will find get_gravity() still returns (0, -9.8, 0) which doesn't match what document says;
4. Now change the character type to RigidBody3D, observe get_gravity() again, you will find the result is the combined calculation of project setting and area setting. It matches what document says.

Link to the document:
https://docs.godotengine.org/en/stable/classes/class_physicsbody3d.html#class-physicsbody3d-method-get-gravity

Im working on creating a custom format using ResourceFormatSaver and ResourceFormatLoader, those are being register in the EditorPlugin. summary: Fix crashes on Adreno 5XX GPUs when scenes become occluded

description.text:
Testing revealed that Android devices with Adreno 5XX GPUs (confirmed on Android 9 and 10, other versions unknown) experience application crashes when scenes containing shaders become invisible relative to the screen (e.g., when the UI is occluded or the app is backgrounded).

The root cause is shader object lifecycle corruption when the EGL context is preserved across surface destroy/create cycles, which occurs when the rendering surface is temporarily destroyed while the context remains active.

This commit implements the following workarounds:

- OpenGL ES path: Disable EGL context preservation for Adreno 5XX GPUs to force full context recreation, preventing shader object corruption.

- Vulkan path: Force fallback to OpenGL3 rendering for Adreno 5XX GPUs, as these devices have critical Vulkan driver bugs that cause crashes in FinalizeDescriptors when handling descriptor sets for occluded UI elements.

- Vulkan thread: Force thread restart on surface loss for problematic Adreno GPUs to ensure clean state recovery.

Affected SoCs include Snapdragon 625 (Adreno 506), Snapdragon 430/435 (Adreno 505), Snapdragon 425 (Adreno 505), Snapdragon 652/653/650 (Adreno 510), and Snapdragon 660/636 (Adreno 509/512).

Testing confirms that crashes no longer occur with these workarounds in place.

summary: Add `@display_if` annotation to GDScript

description.text:
# Overview

This PR allows you to conditionally hide/show exported properties based on a bool with a new `@export_if` annotation:
```gdscript
# Before (needs @tool):
@export var is_alive : bool:
	set(value):
		is_alive = value
		notify_property_list_changed()
@export var health: int

func _validate_property(property: Dictionary):
	if property.name == "number" and not is_alive:
		property.usage |= ~PROPERTY_USAGE_EDITOR


# After (doesn't need @tool):
@export var is_alive := true
@export @display_if("is_alive") var health: int
```

It works by adding "condition: {argument}"  `hint_string`. Then in an `EditorInspectorPlugin` (alongside an `EditorPlugin`), we use `get()` on the object to obtain the condition, if it doesn't exist or isn't a `bool`, it fails and throws an error. Then, if the condition is true, we hide the property's associated `EditorProperty`.

The argument has to be the name of a boolean that's declared (either via static typing or by assigning a default value). Passing a `bool` instead of a `String` is not possible, more info on this in the [proposal (comment)](https://github.com/godotengine/godot-proposals/issues/9842#issuecomment-2136587725).

Closes godotengine/godot-proposals#9842

## Known issues

- Doing the equivalent of `@display_if` with `[Export(hintString : "condition: ConditionName")]` in C# does not properly hide certain data types (Node, Resource, typed Array, typed Dictionary and possibly more)

# TODO

- [ ] Find a way to automatically update the inspector instead of having to click away (need help on this one)
- [ ] Investigate possibility of adding a `[ExportIf(bool condition)]` attribute in C#, possibly in another PR
- [ ] Figure out if autocompletion is possible, and if so how (haven't looked into this yet)
- [ ] Add this to properties exported by the editor where it fits

summary: [PopupMenu] menu position and menu mouse position return values change when per_pixel_transparency is allowed

description.text: ### Tested versions Reproducible in 4.5.x and higher. ### System information MacOS 14.7.5 ### Issue description For `PopupMenu`, `per_pixel_transparency` is affecting return values of at least `get_position` and also `get_mouse_position` as compared to when `per_pixel_transparency` is disabled. The amount different from each other appears to be a multiple of `theme_cache.panel_style->get_margin` for top, left, etc., where the theme margins wind up being applied at least twice. This issue arose while fixing an issue with `PopupMenu` (#110256) where a state exists that flickers submenus. The cause of that was incorrectly placed autohide areas and mouse position testing that were not adjusted for theme margins. While I might be able to work out a way to reverse-out the doubled theme margins when `per_pixel_transparency` is enabled, it would be a lot cleaner to fix the underlying issue, which I think must be in `DisplayServer`. In the screen recordings below, the red area is the `autohide_area` that closes the submenu if the mouse enters it, and the green line extends to the `Point2D` in the code that determines if the mouse is within the area. Here is the behavior in the master. Note that both the autohide area and mouse detection point are incorrect. You can see there is a place where the autohide area overlaps the menu item. This allows the flickering state where the mouse point can enter that area which hides the submenu, but then immediately reopens because the mouse is still inside the menu item. The other confounding part of this is that the code to determine whether the mouse is over a menu item computes a different point from the autohide area detection code. Master - note the black gap below the menu item should not be there, and the end of the green line does not end at the tip of the cursor like it should: https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/5d77a01e-9918-44db-999d-ba44af029b90 Here's the editor with `per_pixel_transparency` disabled with fixed code where the autohide area is in the right location and the mouse point is at the cursor tip: https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/b45105eb-8cc0-4d99-9c7e-94c70fc33b10 Here's the editor with the same fixed code, but only with `per_pixel_transparency` enabled. Note in the upper left corner of the autohide area the shift that appears to be 2X theme margin, and that the green line is offset from the cursor by the same amount. https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/a40072da-d0fe-4b26-b672-4227fb3fee8c Here's a confusing result - when `per_pixel_transparency` is enabled for a running project with the fix, only the autohide area is offset, not the mouse point. https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/961dee89-98ab-4117-9bde-b5fd1194beb9 ### Steps to reproduce You'll need to have a debug draw on the autohide area to visualize the offset, probably easiest in `_draw_items`. Here's the declaration for it, and it contains one or two `Rect2`, the top and bottom area. ``` List autohide_areas; ``` ### Minimal reproduction project (MRP) [Edited] Here is a place in `_activate_submenu` where the `Rect2` value of `this_rect` changes when `per_pixel_transparency` changes. ``` const Point2 panel_ofs_start = Point2(panel->get_offset(SIDE_LEFT), panel->get_offset(SIDE_TOP)) * win_scale; const Point2 panel_ofs_end = Point2(-panel->get_offset(SIDE_RIGHT), -panel->get_offset(SIDE_BOTTOM)) * win_scale; const Point2 this_pos = get_position() + Point2(0, panel_ofs_start.y + theme_cache.panel_style->get_margin(SIDE_TOP) * win_scale); Rect2 this_rect(this_pos, get_size()); print_line("ORIG THIS RECT", this_rect); ``` The theme_cache and `win_scale` values do not change, so the wrong value seems to be returned by `panel->get_offset`

summary: Update rect after `_pre_popup` in `popup_centered_*`.

description.text:
Fixes https://github.com/godotengine/godot/issues/112062

`_pre_popup` can change window size, and therefore, but `popup_centered` was overwriting it immediately after, now it's recalculating position instead.
